column8: Lauren pulled a ten-pound note from her pocket but as she did so it suddenly ripped down the middle. "Oh no, I don't believe it, it's ruined! And now I____________ten pounds!" she said to her friend, Pete. (LOSE)
column9: "Why don't you tape the two pieces back together? I'm sure it_____________fine," Pete replied. Lauren had her doubts about this, but she decided to try it. (BE)
column10: She got some tape from her desk and taped the note together as Pete had suggested. But an hour later, when she tired to buy a sandwich, the shop owner refused her note. When she asked why, the surly shop owner said, "Sorry, but sometimes two halves____________a whole." (NOT MAKE)
column11: Russian vinaigrette is a type of salad common in Russia, which consist of diced carrots, beetroot and potatoes as well as chopped onions and sometimes sauerkraut. Vinaigrette dressing, from which the salad gets_________name, is poured over the top. (IT)
column12: The salad was adapted from European recipes that were popular in the 19th century. However, it is now_________eaten in Russia than in western Europe. (WIDELY)
column13: The salad often appears at celebrations alongside other small dishes, such as cold cuts, cheeses, canapes and open sandwiches. Usually_____________dishes are laid on the table before the guests arrive. (THIS)
column14: The colourful salad is low in calories and full of nutrients, so it_________________for anyone who is also on a diet or just keen to eat healthy. (RECOMMEND)
